title: lora_l2.c: RX thread reads lora_dev without synchronization during potential init race
description:
## Location
firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:206

## Issue
The RX thread accesses lora_state.lora_dev without holding lora_mutex:

The RX thread loop calls lora_recv(lora_state.lora_dev, ...) without mutex protection.

While lora_dev is documented as set once during init, there is a race window:
1. lichen_lora_l2_start() acquires mutex at line 334
2. lichen_lora_l2_start() sets running = 1 at line 367
3. lichen_lora_l2_start() starts RX thread at line 372
4. RX thread starts and reads lora_state.running (sees 1)
5. RX thread reads lora_state.lora_dev
6. lichen_lora_l2_start() releases mutex at line 382

In this window, the RX thread reads lora_dev while the calling thread still holds the mutex.

## What goes wrong
- Currently benign because lora_dev is set in init(), not start(), and init() must complete before start() can succeed
- However, the code comment at line 93-98 says lora_dev is Mutex-protected: device pointer set once during init but the RX thread violates this by reading without mutex
- A future refactor that moves device initialization into start() would introduce a real data race
- The inconsistency between the documented access pattern and actual code creates maintainer confusion

## Fix
Either:
1. Read lora_dev under mutex once at RX thread start and cache it as a local variable, or
2. Add a comment clarifying that the mutex documentation is for writers only and RX thread relies on the init-before-start invariant
